subject: Mathematics
title: Shadow Story

sections.heading: Shadow Story
sections.content:
Nakul and his friends saw a shadow play known as Togalu
Gombeyaata in a village fair. They all were fascinated to see
the shadows.
They also wanted to make
shadows. So, they made
different animal shadows on
the wall with their fingers.
You can also try to make the
shadows.
Share information about Togalu Gombeyaata with children which is a
puppetry art form practised in Karnataka. The puppets are used
to depict scenes from Indian epics. Let children also share their
experiences of watching a puppet show.
Shadow Story
(Togalu)
How are
these
shadows
forming?
Wow! These
shadows
are so
amazing.
Reprint 2026-27
Now put the torch light on the objects around you and
observe the shapes of their shadows.
A. Will the size of shadows change on changing the position
of the torch?
B. At what time of the day is your shadow longest?
C. At what time of the day is your shadow shortest?
Fun with Shadows
A. Have you seen your own shadow or the shadow of a tree,
a dog, a cow or any other animal?
B. When do we see a shadow?
C. When does a shadow vanish?
Let us Talk
Do a role-play by making the shadows of different animals
and creating their sounds.
Let us Do
Discuss with children about their observations on their shadows
at different times of the day and the reasons behind it.
Reprint 2026-27
A. Trace the shape of the objects around you. For example,
eraser, leaf, matchbox, sharpener, pencil colour, etc.
B. Draw the shapes you get after tracing.
Let us Think
Let us Trace
Write the names of objects that have the same trace as:
A. Bottle cap
B. Eraser
Reprint 2026-27
Hide and Seek
Circle and rectangle are playing hide and seek.
Rectangle is hiding from the circle.
Where should
I hide? Oh!
Shoe box
1, 2, 3... 10
I am coming.
I look like the
face of the shoe
box. I will hide
behind it.
Where are
you? Are you
behind the
photo frame?
Reprint 2026-27
Where should
I hide?
The door bell
rang, Triangle
and Square came
and joined the
game.
Where are you?
Are you behind
the TV?
I caught you! You
are behind the
shoe box.
It is my turn
now 1, 2, 3...10
Reprint 2026-27
Colour the Carpet
Make your own design with different shapes and colour
them.
Colour the Triangles red.
Colour the Circles green.
Colour the Rectangles
yellow.
Colour the Squares blue.
They all are playing together now.
A. Circle can hide in the clock, ball, ________, ________.
B. Rectangle can hide in the shoe box, photo frame,
_________, _________.
C. But Triangle and Square are confused and wonder
where to hide now. Suggest them some places where
they can hide.
i. Triangle can hide in _________, _________.
ii. Square can hide in _________, _________.
Reprint 2026-27
Catch the Corner!
Naveen and his friends are playing a game. Naveen is standing
facing the wall and clapping. His friends are running along
the table as he claps. When he stops clapping, everybody
stops. The child who is not at a corner will be out. Then the
child will clap and the game will go on.
Look at the picture and answer the following questions.
A. Name the children standing near the corners of the table.
B. Can you tell who will clap next?
C. Where is Wasim standing?
D. Can this game be played around a round table? Why or
why not?
E. Name the objects around us that have straight edges.
F. Many objects around us have curved edges. Some are
shown below. Explore and share the names of such
objects that you see around.
Udit
Paramjeet 
Wasim 
Naysa
Seema
Naveen
Reprint 2026-27
Ask children to explore various objects around them and ask
them to find out the number of their edges, corners and sides.
Origami Fun!
A. Take a paper and fold it to make it a square.
B. It has corners and edges.
C. Fold it in half by joining two corners as
shown in the picture.
D. Tick ü the shape which you get.
i. ii. iii.
E. This shape has corners and
edges.
F. Fold the paper again into half to get a
triangle and then unfold it.
G. Now make two triangles from two
corners of the triangle.
H. How many corners are there now?
I. Fold the third corner upwards and make
the nose and eyes as shown.
Your paper dog is ready!
A. How many corners do objects with curved edges have?
B. Name the objects that have both straight and curved
edges?
Can you fold the square sheet in such a way so as to get a
shape with 4 sides? How many corners does it have now?
Let us Think
Reprint 2026-27
Patterns
A. Make designs by stamping objects having different
shapes like — bottle caps, erasers, pencils, coins, etc.
B. Radha is applying mehandi on her hands. You can also
trace your hand to make a mehandi pattern or design.
Reprint 2026-27
Nitya is playing with leaves and making patterns.
Now you also collect some leaves and make a pattern. Paint
one side of the leaf and print it on the space given below.
Let us Do
Project Work
Make a wall hanging using cutouts of different shapes with the help of
your elders to decorate your home or classroom.
Reprint 2026-27
Extend the following patterns.

sections.heading: AB BC CD
sections.content:
AA BB CC DD EE
A.
B.
C.
D.
E.
F.
G.
H.
I.
Reprint 2026-27
Kikku’s mother also jumps on the 6th tile. Help her by
putting a × on the next tiles.
1, 6, 11, _____, _____, _____, _____, _____
Extend the following number patterns.
A. 5, 10, 15,
B. 7, 14, 21,
C. 1, 7, 13,
13 14 15 16 
17 18
242526
Let us Do
Kikku rabbit jumps on the 4th tile. He forgot where to jump
next. Help him by drawing a circle on the next tiles.
1, 4, _____, _____, _____, _____, _____, _____
Reprint 2026-27

learningObjectives: Understand the key concepts of Shadow Story
Identify and explain the main ideas covered in this chapter
Apply knowledge from this chapter to real-world situations
Recall important terms and definitions from Shadow Story
